description: A cognitive psychology laboratory has collected classic visual search data (Treisman & Gelade, 1980 paradigm) at ~/pebl/data/visual_search_data.csv. Calculate the reaction time search slopes (ms per item) for four search types (feature_present, feature_absent, conjunction_present, conjunction_absent) for each valid participant and the group averages. Analysis requires excluding trials where response_correct is 0 and excluding any participant whose overall accuracy on target_present trials is less than 20%. For each valid participant and search type, calculate the mean RT at each set_size and determine the slope using linear regression (mean_RT ~ set_size). Write the results to ~/pebl/analysis/visual_search_report.json including a 'participants' list (each entry containing 'id', 'slopes_ms_per_item' dictionary, and 'excluded' status with 'reason' if applicable) and a 'group_mean_slopes' dictionary containing the four group averages.
